questions.question: What is Lexical's EditorState?
options: A mutable DOM representation of editor content
An immutable snapshot of all editor content at a point in time
A Redux reducer for editor actions
The HTML string of the editor's current content
questions.answer: An immutable snapshot of all editor content at a point in time
questions.explanation: EditorState is immutable. Every change produces a new EditorState. You read it with editor.getEditorState().read(() => {...}) and mutate via editor.update(() => {...}).

questions.question: How do you programmatically modify Lexical editor content?
questions.explanation: Mutations must be inside editor.update(). Lexical creates a new EditorState from the mutation. Mutations outside update() will throw or be silently ignored.

questions.question: What are the two data representations Meridian uses for page content?
options: Yjs Y.Doc (live CRDT) and blocks table (persistent PostgreSQL records)
questions.answer: Yjs Y.Doc (live CRDT) and blocks table (persistent PostgreSQL records)
questions.explanation: The Y.Doc is the live collaborative editing state in memory. The blocks table is the durable, queryable record in PostgreSQL. syncPageBlocks() reconciles them.

questions.question: Explain the purpose of the `block_versions` table and what triggers a new version entry.
questions.answer_key: block_versions stores the full content history of each block as JSONB snapshots. A new row is inserted when a block's content changes (after a debounce threshold to avoid storing every keystroke). It enables version history, undo, and content auditing.
questions.rubric: Full credit: explains content history purpose, JSONB snapshot, debounce pattern. Partial: explains history but not the debounce mechanism.

questions.question: What role does Yjs play in the Meridian editor, and how does it relate to Lexical?
questions.answer_key: Yjs is a CRDT (Conflict-free Replicated Data Type) library. The Y.Doc is the shared collaborative document state. It is connected to Lexical via a Yjs binding plugin that maps Lexical nodes to Yjs types. The WebSocket provider synchronizes the Y.Doc between all connected clients in real-time.
questions.rubric: Full credit: explains CRDT, Y.Doc as shared state, Lexical binding, WebSocket sync. Partial: explains sync without the binding mechanism.
